description: scan_vault_feed (feed.rs:323-358) uses std::fs::read_dir which returns directory entries in arbitrary filesystem-dependent order (not sorted). This means visible_note_ids is populated in non-deterministic order between different invocations. When select_past_note or confirm_note_deletion triggers a re-scan, the emitted snapshot may present notes in a different order than the previous snapshot, causing the TS client's feed list to visually reorder on every note click or deletion. REQ-FEED-022 does not mandate a sort order for feed_initial_state, but REQ-FEED-017 requires the feed to 'ソート順を再計算' (recalculate sort order) after save events — implying the Rust side is expected to provide a consistent ordering. scan_vault_feed provides no sort guarantee. The unit test make_note_deleted_snapshot_populates_remaining_feed_after_deletion (feed.rs:715-740) uses iter().any() which is order-agnostic, so this defect is not caught. No test verifies that successive scan_vault_feed calls on the same vault return the same note order.

evidence.snippet:
for entry in read_dir.flatten() {
    // No sort applied — filesystem-dependent order

crossReference: promptnotes/src-tauri/src/feed.rs:714-740 — test uses any() not ordered assertion
resolution: Sort visible_note_ids before returning from scan_vault_feed, using a deterministic key (e.g., note_id string sort or updatedAt descending from metadata). Add a test asserting that scan_vault_feed on a multi-file vault returns a consistent, sorted order across multiple calls.
